name: lower-third-corporate
description: Broadcast-style corporate lower third for 1920x1080 HD. Displays a name and title/role with a dark semi-transparent background bar, vertical accent bar, and staggered text reveal. Use for interview identifiers, speaker names, documentary IDs, or any broadcast lower third context.

design_notes.palette: Standard Corporate Dark Stack from research § 3.1. Background #0B0B0F (near-black, cool blue cast), accent #3B82F6 (tech blue), primary text #F9FAFB, secondary text #94A3B8.
design_notes.safe_area: Lower third zone sits within y:810–1000, respecting the 96px outer margin (5% safe) standard. Left edge of text at x=192 = 10% inner margin safe area.
design_notes.easing: easeOutExpo (0.19, 1, 0.22, 1) for all entrances from off-screen. easeInExpo (0.95, 0.05, 0.795, 0.035) for all exits. Per research §1.1, these are the snappiest professional curves for on/off-screen motion.
design_notes.stagger: 0.15s (≈4.5 frames @29.97fps) between text layers — standard content-section stagger from §1.5. Exits are 25% faster than entrances per the §1.2 rule.
design_notes.timing: Entry animation 0.5s, hold 3–5s, exit animation 0.4s. Lower-third slide target: 10–18 frames at 24fps (§1.2), adjusted to 29.97fps = ~0.5s entrance.

name_text.description: Primary name displayed on the lower third

title_text.description: Job title, role, or descriptor displayed below the name

accent_color.description: RGB 0–1 range. Default is #3B82F6 tech blue from corporate palette. Change to brand color.

hold_duration.description: Seconds the lower third stays fully visible. Increase for longer reads. Animation exit begins at time = hold_duration + 1.0.

x_position.description: X position of the left edge of the graphic. 192px = 10% inner safe margin. Increase to move right.

steps.note: Background color matches #0B0B0F (0.043, 0.043, 0.059) — the corporate near-black with cool blue cast from §3.1. Using slightly tinted black rather than pure black prevents harsh contrast and is the professional dark-theme standard (Material Design §3.1).

Master position controller NULL. Parenting all lower-third layers to this null allows the entire graphic to be repositioned with one value. Per the template best-practice in lower-third.md: 'Parent all layers to the NULL to move the entire lower third with one position control.'
NULL anchor at origin [0,0] so child layers animate relative to their own positions. The NULL is the global offset handle — offsetting it later moves everything uniformly.

Background semi-transparent bar. Near-black #0B0B0F at 85% opacity so the underlying video is subtly visible — the professional standard from lower-third.md: 'Keep Background Pill opacity at 85% (not 100%) so underlying video is subtly visible — this feels more professional and less heavy.'
Shape group container for the rectangle and its fill/transform properties.

BG bar width 560px accommodates typical name + title text. Height 96px = comfortable padding for two lines of text at 42pt + 28pt with 12px gap. Positioned so its center is at x=472 from layer anchor, placing the left edge at ~192px from the frame left edge (10% inner safe margin).
Fill color #0B0B0F = [0.043, 0.043, 0.059] in 0-1 RGB. Opacity 85% per the professional guideline — see lower-third.md tips. No stroke needed on background element.

Position center at [472, 876]: y=876 places bar center in the lower-third zone (y:810–1000 per §3.2 broadcast grid). The 1920×1080 key zone 'Lower Third' is y:810–1080. x=472 centers the 560px bar starting at x=192 (10% inner safe margin).

Vertical accent bar in tech blue (#3B82F6). Provides visual hierarchy signal and brand color pop. Standard lower third accent bar technique — the bright vertical stripe draws the eye and anchors the left edge.
Shape group for accent bar rectangle.

4px wide × 72px tall accent bar. Width 4px is the standard thin accent stripe for corporate broadcast (substantial enough to read on screen, thin enough to remain subtle). Height 72px fits within the 96px BG bar with 12px top/bottom padding.
Accent color #3B82F6 = [0.231, 0.510, 0.965] — 'Standard tech blue' from the Corporate/Tech dark stack in §3.1. Full opacity for maximum pop against the near-black background.

Position accent bar at x=200 (8px inside the 192px left safe margin, giving it slight inset). Anchor point at bottom-center [0, 36] so scale-Y animation grows from the bottom up, visually 'drawing on' from below.

Name text at 42pt Bold. Per §3.3 type scale: 'Lower third name: 32–42px, Bold'. Inter Bold chosen as the accessible workhorse sans-serif per §3.3. Color #F9FAFB = [0.976, 0.980, 0.984] — warm white from corporate dark stack (easier on eyes than pure white). Tracking 0 is correct for this size per the tracking table in §3.3.

Name text positioned at x=224 (24px right of the accent bar, creating breathing room between accent and text). y=862 places baseline above the bar center. Left-anchored so the text grows rightward and stays flush to the safe margin.

Title at 26pt Regular. Per §3.3 type scale: 'Lower third role: 20–28px, Regular/Light'. Color #94A3B8 = [0.580, 0.639, 0.722] — 'Muted cool gray / Secondary text' from corporate dark stack §3.1, creating clear visual hierarchy below the name. Tracking +30 at subtitle size per §3.3 tracking table: 'Subtitle/secondary (40–60px): +20 to +50'.

Title text at y=896 — 34px below the name text at y=862. At 42pt line height ≈34px, this creates tight but comfortable stacking per §3.3 leading ratios: 'Multi-line display heading: 1.1–1.2×' (42 × 1.15 ≈ 48px, with the gap being the descender space).
Parent BG Bar to NULL controller. All graphic elements parented to NULL so the entire lower third can be repositioned or animated as a unit by moving only the NULL.
Parent Accent Bar to NULL controller.
Parent Name Text to NULL controller.
Parent Title Text to NULL controller.

BG Bar slides in from left. Starts at x=-120 (fully off-screen left edge) and arrives at resting position [472, 876] over 0.5s. Duration 0.5s matches the lower-third slide target from §1.2: '10–18 frames @24fps = 417–750ms'. Entry from off-screen uses easeOutExpo for snappy arrival per §1.1.

easeOut applied to BG Bar position keyframes. Per §1.1 rule: 'For elements entering FROM off-screen, do NOT ease out of the first keyframe. Start at full speed, ease into the landing.' ease_out = easeOutExpo behavior: steep initial speed that decelerates sharply on arrival.

Accent bar scale-Y wipe-in. Starts at [100, 0] (collapsed flat) and grows to [100, 100] (full size) between t=0.9–1.25s. Scale-Y from bottom (anchor set at bottom center in step 13) creates a 'drawing on' effect. Timed 0.4s after BG bar arrives (t=1.0) so the sequence reads: background arrives → accent wipes in → text fades. This is the stagger principle from §1.5.
easeOut on accent bar scale for snappy wipe-in. The bar should pop in with energy — ease_out gives it a fast initial growth that decelerates to a clean stop.

Name text opacity fade-in from t=1.1–1.5s (0.4s duration). Starts 0.1s after accent bar begins wipe-in. Per §1.2 text reveals: 'Word fade-in: 4–8 frames @24fps = 167–333ms'; at 29.97fps, 0.4s ≈ 12 frames — matches 'line reveal (slide in)' range. The 0.15s stagger between BG (t=1.0) → Name (t=1.1) → Title (t=1.25) creates the cascade feel.
easeOut on name text opacity. Text fades in with ease_out — starts fast, settles softly to full opacity. Consistent with professional entrance easing standard.

Title text opacity fade-in from t=1.25–1.65s. Deliberately delayed 0.15s after name text starts (t=1.1 + 0.15 = 1.25). This is the §1.5 stagger: 'Content sections: 4–8 frames stagger, 167–333ms — Deliberate, readable.' The 0.15s (≈4.5 frames @29.97fps) stagger between name and title reads as intentional hierarchy, not accident.
easeOut on title text opacity. Matches the entry easing family of all other layers for visual consistency.

Title text exit fade-out. Title exits first, reversing the stagger order (title exits before name). Duration 0.3s — 25% faster than the 0.4s entrance per §1.2 rule: 'Exits should be 20–30% faster than entrances.' This creates the layered collapse sequence: title → name → accent → background.

easeIn on title exit opacity. Per §1.1: 'For elements EXITING to off-screen, do NOT ease into the last keyframe. Accelerate the whole way out.' ease_in = easeInExpo curve for dramatic, snappy exit acceleration.

Name text exit fade-out, offset 0.15s after title exit starts (5.4 + 0.15 = 5.55). Reverse stagger: title out → name out → graphic out. Same 0.3s duration as title exit. The layered exit reinforces the same visual rhythm as the entrance.
easeIn on name text exit opacity. Accelerates out to nothing. Consistent exit easing family.

Accent bar scale-Y collapse. Reverses the wipe-in at exit. Collapses from bottom (anchor at bottom, so bar wipes OUT downward). Duration 0.25s — fastest element in the exit sequence. The accent bar was last to appear after BG, so it exits before BG to maintain visual order.
easeIn on accent bar exit scale — accelerates the collapse to zero, creating a snappy wipe-out.

BG Bar slides out to the left. Exits from rest position [472, 876] to off-screen [-120, 876] over 0.45s. Exit is 0.45s vs 0.5s entrance — approximately 10% faster, within the §1.2 range of 20–30% faster for exits. Uses easeInExpo for a dramatic departure per §1.1: 'easeInExpo (0.95, 0.05, 0.795, 0.035) — dramatic exit'.
easeIn on BG bar exit position. Per §1.1 exit rule: start slow and accelerate the whole way out — the bar builds speed as it slides left, disappearing with momentum.
